app.name: SaúdeFoz
app.tagline: Access Brazil's public health system (SUS) in Foz do Iguaçu. Official information in your language.
app.disclaimer: For guidance only. Does not replace medical care. Emergency: call SAMU 192.

section.ptNoticeBody: This content comes from Brazil's Ministry of Health and is in Portuguese. Use the translate button to read it in your language.

ubs.noResults: No results found. Try a different term.

hero.name: Ponte Saúde
hero.tagline: Connecting migrants to the SUS, promoting health and dignity.
hero.intro: SaúdeFoz is the digital tool of Ponte Saúde: clear, multilingual and accessible SUS information, so that no migrant goes without care simply for not knowing how or where to find it.

context.title: Context
context.body: Foz do Iguaçu is one of Brazil's most diverse cities, with more than 90 nationalities and a growing migrant population, right on the Triple Frontier.
context.stat1Label: care visits by migrants in Foz do Iguaçu's public health network between 2023 and 2024.
context.stat2Label: nationalities living side by side in the Triple Frontier region.

problem.title: The problem
problem.body: Migrants face real barriers to effectively accessing the health system, resulting in care that is late, fragmented or absent.
problem.cause1Body: Limited communication between health professionals and migrants hinders dialogue, attentive listening and understanding of guidance.
problem.cause2Body: The lack of a CPF, personal documents or proof of residence, together with unfamiliarity with the procedures, makes registration and access to services difficult.
problem.cause3Body: Available services are poorly connected across health, social assistance and civil society, with fragmented processes and lost follow-up.

solution.title: Our solution
solution.name: Ponte Saúde
solution.intro: Three fronts working together to build a bridge between the migrant community and the health system.
solution.pillar1Body: Trained bilingual migrants who act as cultural mediators: they explain rights and support people in accessing the UBS health centers.
solution.pillar2Body: Physical and digital tools to overcome the language barrier: visual materials, audio in several languages and a navigation assistant for using the SUS.
solution.pillar2Badge: You're using this tool right now
solution.pillar3Body: Preventive care spaces with screening, health education and timely referral to primary care.

impact.title: The impact we're after
impact.intro: A health system that is fairer, more efficient and adapted to cultural diversity.
impact.item1: Better access to health information and easier communication across languages.
impact.item2: More migrants seen in primary care, not only in emergencies.
impact.item3: Fewer avoidable emergencies and less strain on urgent-care services.
impact.item4: Higher vaccination coverage and uptake of preventive programs.
impact.item5: More accurate epidemiological data and greater equity and inclusion in health.

project.footerNote: Ponte Saúde has no official affiliation with the Brazilian government or health institutions. It grows from joint work with UBS health centers, social organizations and volunteers to strengthen access and continuity of care.

about.title: About SaúdeFoz
about.paragraph1: SaúdeFoz is a non-profit project that helps immigrants in Foz do Iguaçu and the Triple Frontier region access Brazil's Unified Health System (SUS).
about.paragraph2: All information comes from official sources at Brazil's Ministry of Health (gov.br) and is updated automatically every week.
about.paragraph3: This project has no official affiliation with the Brazilian government or any health institution.
about.disclaimerBody: This website is for information only. It does not replace professional medical care. In an emergency, call SAMU 192 or go to the nearest UPA.
